title: Record Your First Sample
description: Learn how to sample external audio into the EP-133 and play it back using the pads.

steps.title: Connect Your Audio Source
steps.instruction: Connect your audio source (phone, tablet, turntable, or synthesizer) to the orange INPUT jack using a 3.5mm stereo cable.
steps.tip: Use a stereo cable for best results. If sampling from a phone, use a cable without a microphone (TRRS to TRS).

steps.title: Enter Sampling Mode
steps.instruction: Press the SAMPLE/CHOP button to enter sampling mode. The display will show input level meters.

steps.title: Adjust Input Gain
steps.instruction: Turn the orange GAIN knob while your audio source plays. Watch the input meter on the display and aim for a strong signal without clipping (red).

substeps.instruction: Start with GAIN at 12 o'clock position
Play your source audio at the loudest part
Adjust until peaks reach about 80% of the meter

steps.tip: If the signal clips, turn GAIN down. If the meter barely moves, turn GAIN up.

steps.title: Select a Sound Slot
steps.instruction: Use the number keys to enter a sound number (0-999), then press ENTER to select that slot for your new sample.
steps.tip: Start with a low number like 1. If the slot already has a sample, it will be replaced.

steps.title: Record the Sample
steps.instruction: Press RECORD to arm recording. Recording starts automatically when audio is detected (threshold mode), or press any pad to start manually. Press RECORD again to stop.

substeps.instruction: Press RECORD - it will blink, indicating armed
Play your audio source - recording starts automatically
Press RECORD again when you have enough material

steps.warning: Watch your remaining memory on the display. Long samples use more of the 64 MB available.

steps.title: Play Your Sample
steps.instruction: Press MAIN/COMMIT to return to the main view. Now press the pad corresponding to your sound number (e.g., pad 1 for sound 1). Your sample plays!
steps.tip: The sample is automatically saved to RAM. Hold MAIN/COMMIT to permanently save (commit) your work.

outcomes: Successfully recorded a sample into the EP-133
Understanding of the sampling workflow and gain staging
Ability to play back samples using the pads
